Utility functions for handling and verifying ANCS notification and app attribute responses.

# Purpose
This C header file provides utility functions for handling Apple Notification Center Service (ANCS) attributes. It includes functions to determine the length of a notification attribute response ([`ancs_util_get_notif_attr_response_len`](<#ancs_util_get_notif_attr_response_len>)), check if a notification attribute response is complete ([`ancs_util_is_complete_notif_attr_response`](<#ancs_util_is_complete_notif_attr_response>)), and verify if an application attribute dictionary is complete ([`ancs_util_is_complete_app_attr_dict`](<#ancs_util_is_complete_app_attr_dict>)). Additionally, it offers a function to extract pointers to the start of each attribute in a list ([`ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs`](<#ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs>)). These functions help in parsing and validating ANCS data, ensuring that all required attributes are present and correctly formatted. The file includes necessary headers and uses the `#pragma once` directive to prevent multiple inclusions.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `ancs_types.h`
- `services/normal/notifications/notifications.h`
- `stdbool.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### ancs\_util\_is\_complete\_notif\_attr\_response<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ancs_util_is_complete_notif_attr_response}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs_util.h#L29>)

Checks if a notification attribute response is complete.
- **Description**: Use this function to verify if a notification attribute response contains all required attributes and is complete. Call this function when you have received a notification attribute response and need to ensure its completeness before further processing. The function requires a valid pointer for `out_error` to indicate if an error occurred during parsing. It returns false if the response is incomplete or if the length is insufficient to contain the necessary header.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to the notification attribute response data. Must not be null and should point to a valid memory location containing the response.
    - `length`: Size of the data in bytes. Must be greater than the size of the header to proceed with attribute checking.
    - `out_error`: Pointer to a boolean that will be set to true if an error occurs during parsing. Must not be null. The caller must check this value to determine if an error occurred.
- **Output**: Returns true if all required attributes are present and complete; returns false if the response is incomplete or if an error occurs.
- **See Also**: [`ancs_util_is_complete_notif_attr_response`](<ancs_util.c.md#ancs_util_is_complete_notif_attr_response>)  (Implementation)

### ancs\_util\_is\_complete\_app\_attr\_dict<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ancs_util_is_complete_app_attr_dict}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs_util.h#L30>)

Checks if all application attributes are present in the data.
- **Description**: Use this function to verify that a given data buffer contains a complete set of application attributes. This function is useful when you need to ensure that all expected attributes are present before processing the data further. It must be called with a valid pointer for `out_error`, which will be set to indicate if the data is invalid. The function returns a boolean indicating the presence of all attributes.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: A pointer to the data buffer containing application attributes. Must not be null.
    - `length`: The size of the data buffer in bytes. Must be greater than zero.
    - `out_error`: A pointer to a boolean that will be set to true if the data is invalid or cannot be parsed. Must not be null.
- **Output**: Returns true if all requested attributes are present and complete; false if one or more attributes are missing or the last attribute is truncated.
- **See Also**: [`ancs_util_is_complete_app_attr_dict`](<ancs_util.c.md#ancs_util_is_complete_app_attr_dict>)  (Implementation)

### ancs\_util\_get\_attr\_ptrs<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs_util.h#L37>)

Extract pointers to specified attributes from a data buffer.
- **Description**: Use this function to extract pointers to attributes specified in `attr_list` from a given data buffer. It checks if all requested attributes are present and complete. If the data buffer is invalid or any attribute is missing or truncated, it sets `out_error` to true and returns false. Ensure that `out_error` is not null before calling this function. This function is useful when you need to parse and validate attribute data from a buffer.
- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: Pointer to the data buffer containing attributes. Must not be null.
    - `length`: Size of the data buffer in bytes. Must be at least the size of one `ANCSAttribute`.
    - `attr_list`: Array of `FetchedAttribute` structures specifying the attributes to extract. Must not be null.
    - `num_attrs`: Number of attributes in `attr_list`. Must be non-negative.
    - `out_attr_ptrs`: Array to store pointers to the extracted attributes. Can be null if pointers are not needed.
    - `out_error`: Pointer to a boolean that indicates if an error occurred. Must not be null.
- **Output**: Returns true if all requested attributes are present and complete; false if any attribute is missing or the last attribute is truncated.
- **See Also**: [`ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs`](<ancs_util.c.md#ancs_util_get_attr_ptrs>)  (Implementation)
